commit.message:
Add enableSigning config flag for optional signing/verification (#193)

* Add enableSigning config flag to make signing/verification optional (closes #10)

When enableSigning is false (defaults to true for backward compatibility):
- _signAsWriter returns empty string instead of computing signature
- All verification methods return true immediately
- sync() skips signature presence and verification checks
- Topic validators, key update, and load handlers skip signature checks

* Skip topic validator and load signatures when signing disabled

- Skip topic validator registration entirely when enableSigning is false
  to avoid unnecessary per-message decryption overhead
- Skip load request signature computation when signing disabled
- Update JSDoc to document that all signing paths are affected

* Skip raw serialization when signing disabled, add security WARNING

- Conditionally serialize sync messages only when verification is needed
- Add explicit WARNING about security implications of disabling signing
- Document interop incompatibility with signing-enabled peers

* Remove dead enableSigning branch, gate snapshot signing, clarify scope

- Remove unreachable enableSigning check inside already-gated topic validator
- Skip snapshot signature computation when signing is disabled
- Clarify docstring: application-level only, transport signing unaffected

* Skip ACL lookup in load handlers when signing disabled, consistent sync() return

- Short-circuit doc-load and snapshot-load handlers to only fetch writers
  (for requestor identity) when signing disabled, avoiding full ACL lookup
- Return false consistently from sync() on signature rejection (was undefined)

* Use _userPublicKey as requestor when signing disabled, skip ACL lookup

Avoids undefined requestor when writers list is empty during bootstrap,
and eliminates unnecessary ACL call in the signing-disabled path.

* Address review feedback on enableSigning flag

- Add _isSigningEnabled() private helper to centralize config checks
  and reduce drift across call sites
- Refactor doc-load and snapshot-load handlers to use boolean
  `authorized` flag instead of overloading `requestor` variable
- Add empty signature guards in load handlers to cleanly reject
  requests from peers with signing disabled
- Make sync() return type explicit (Promise<boolean>) with
  consistent `return true` on success and `return false` on all
  auth failures
- Replace all inline enableSigning config checks with the helper

* Fix sync() review comments: check load result, guard snapshot signing, document return type

- Check sync() return value in load-response path and return false to try next peer on failure
- Skip snapshot serialization and signature verification when signing is disabled
- Add @since JSDoc annotation documenting the Promise<boolean> return type change

* Address PR #193 review comments: fix JSDoc and add security warnings

- Update sync() JSDoc to acknowledge breaking change from Promise<void>
- Add security warning at snapshot verification bypass when signing disabled
- Expand _sendLoadRequestAndSync() JSDoc to document full return contract

* Clean up topicValidators entry in close() to prevent leaked GossipSub validators

* Update packages/collabswarm/src/collabswarm-document.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix _sendLoadRequestAndSync docstring and add BREAKING CHANGE note to sync()

- Enumerate all false-return cases in _sendLoadRequestAndSync (too-short
  payload, unrecognized keyID) and clarify that only decrypt() failure throws
- Add prominent BREAKING CHANGE callout to sync() JSDoc for the return type
  change from Promise<void> to Promise<boolean>

* Remove incorrect version number from sync() BREAKING CHANGE JSDoc

commit.message:
Add document path validation for namespace enforcement (#192)

* Add document path validation for namespace enforcement (closes #19)

* Use unknown type for userPublicKey, call close() on validation failure

- Change validateDocumentPath callback param from any to unknown
- Call close() before throwing on validation failure to clean up
  pubsub subscriptions and protocol handlers registered by open()

* Wrap validateDocumentPath in try/catch to ensure cleanup on callback throw

* Support async validateDocumentPath, fix null safety, update JSDoc

- Allow callback to return boolean | Promise<boolean>
- Cache callback to narrow null type before calling
- Document that open() may throw on validation failure

* Fix JSDoc: Promise<boolean> return type, document open() throw behavior

* Fix @returns wording for load/open to mention partition case

* Address PR review: fix error wrapping and topic validator cleanup

- Wrap non-Error values in Error() when validateDocumentPath callback
  throws, ensuring consistent error types for callers
- Remove GossipSub topic validator in close() to prevent resource leaks
  when validation fails or document is closed normally

* Move validateDocumentPath before pubsub subscription in open()

Restructure open() so path validation runs before subscribing to pubsub
or registering protocol handlers, preventing temporarily joining an
unauthorized topic. Update docstrings for enableTopicValidators and
validateDocumentPath to document close() cleanup behavior.

* Address PR review round 3: fix open() docstring, cleanup safety, and topic validator

- Update open() JSDoc to document the actual order of operations (load first,
  then validate, then subscribe) and explain the design trade-off of loading
  before registering protocol handlers.
- Clear _pubsubHandler on validation failure so close() won't try to
  unsubscribe from a non-existent subscription.
- Remove enableTopicValidators config gate from close() topic validator
  cleanup — always attempt deletion since Map.delete is a no-op for missing
  keys and this avoids stale validators if config is mutated between
  open() and close().

* Address PR review round 4: fix pubsub handler ordering and add doc notes

- Move _pubsubHandler assignment to after validation succeeds so close()
  won't try to unsubscribe if open() failed during validation
- Document the race window between load() and subscribe() in open() JSDoc
- Document that multiple document instances sharing the same path is not
  supported in close() JSDoc

* Add validateDocumentPath tests and clarify docstring error semantics

- Add 6 unit tests covering validateDocumentPath control flow: returns
  true, returns false, throws Error, throws non-Error, undefined
  (skipped), and async Promise<boolean> variants.
- Update validateDocumentPath docstring to distinguish the two error
  cases: returning false yields a descriptive Error message, while
  throwing rethrows the error as-is (or wraps non-Error values).

* Strengthen open() race-window docs and clarify test rationale

- Expand the Race window JSDoc on open() to explicitly note callers
  should call load() again after open() if they need to ensure no
  messages were missed.
- Add explanatory comment to validateDocumentPath tests clarifying why
  they test the callback contract directly rather than through open().

* Apply suggestions from code review

fix: force TERM=dumb for gh auth PTY, extend timeout to 60s

gh auth login uses bubbletea TUI which emits cursor-positioning
ANSI sequences. With TERM set to a capable value, the fancy UI
draws over itself with escape codes that our sanitizer can't
reliably reconstruct into recognisable strings like
'Login with a web browser'. Setting TERM=dumb + NO_COLOR=1 forces
gh into plain-text fallback mode so the prompt text appears as
literal printable characters.

Also:
- Extend code wait deadline from 30s to 60s
- Log PTY OSErrors at WARNING instead of silently swallowing them
- Dump the sanitised PTY buffer in the timeout warning so failures
  are visible without needing DEBUG logging enabled
- Broaden Stage-1 match: 'web browser' (case-insensitive) in
  addition to exact phrase matches
- Add per-iteration DEBUG log of PTY content for future diagnosis

commit.message:
feat: implement real Copilot API call in /api/ask (0.1.1)

Replace the hardcoded stub response with an actual call to the
GitHub Copilot chat completions API (api.githubcopilot.com) —
the same API that powers the GitHub Copilot CLI.

How it works:
1. The stored GitHub OAuth token (from the auth flow) is exchanged
   for a short-lived Copilot API token via
   api.github.com/copilot_internal/v2/token (cached, auto-refreshed).
2. The prompt + system prompt are sent to
   api.githubcopilot.com/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible).
3. If a Home Assistant MCP server URL is configured, it is included
   in the system prompt so the model knows about it.
4. The real response text is returned to HA Assistant/Assist.

Also:
- Increase api.py ask timeout from 10s to 120s (LLM calls take time)
- Bump to 0.1.1

commit.message:
fix: switch gh auth to device code flow (remove --web)

gh auth login --web forces a PKCE web flow with a localhost redirect
that can never complete inside a headless container. Switch to the
standard interactive device code flow used by GitHub Copilot CLI:

  gh auth login --hostname <host> --git-protocol https ...

The PTY driver now uses a two-stage state machine:
  Stage 1 - detect the 'How would you like to authenticate?' prompt
            and press Enter to select 'Login with a web browser'
  Stage 2 - wait for the device code (XXXX-XXXX), capture it, then
            press Enter so gh opens https://github.com/login/device

The user navigates to github.com/login/device, enters the code, and
gh polls for completion — no localhost redirect needed.

Bump to 0.1.0.6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: clear stale pending auth state with null user_code

Three places were preventing the auth code from ever appearing:

1. Bridge startup: if the persisted auth state file contained a
   pending_device_flow with user_code=null (left from a previous
   crashed/timed-out attempt), it was restored and surfaced to HA
   as 'already pending'. Now cleared on startup.

2. Bridge /auth/device/start: if the gh process was running but
   produced no code, the endpoint returned the null-code state
   unchanged instead of restarting. Now kills and restarts.

3. config_flow: a pending state with user_code=None was treated as
   a valid 'already pending' flow, so /auth/device/start was never
   called. Now only reuses a pending state that has a real code.

Bump to 0.1.0.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wait for one-time code before pressing Enter in gh auth login

The gh auth login --web flow prints the one-time device code first,
then waits for Enter before opening the browser. Previously, Enter was
sent immediately after spawning the process, before any output was
read. This caused the code to never be captured, so the HA UI showed
no code or verification URL.

Fix: poll gh output for up to 20 seconds waiting for the code to
appear. Once captured, send Enter so gh proceeds with the auth flow.
If no code appears within the timeout, send Enter anyway and let
subsequent polls pick it up.

Also bump versions to 0.1.0.4.

commit.message:
Add portability and generated-asset quality gates for VS Code installs (#19)

This change adds repo-level controls to catch environment-specific data
and malformed generated assets before they ship. It also verifies that a
copied WhytCard setup remains installable and structurally valid across
target environments, including the VS Code-specific files the repo
generates.

- **Portability guardrails**
- adds a dedicated `vscode-install-portability` workflow that installs
the generated bundle into a fresh target repo on Ubuntu and Windows
- validates the installed `.github/` and `.vscode/` layout instead of
only checking source files in this repo

- **Generated VS Code asset validation**
- adds `scripts/validate-vscode-output.js` to validate generated agents,
prompts, instructions, hook scripts, and `mcp.json`
- checks frontmatter shape, required hook wiring, expected script set,
unresolved template tokens, and non-portable text patterns
- verifies that the generated prompt list matches the embedded command
allowlist used by the VS Code prompt-dispatch hook

- **CI workflow tightening**
- extends `plugin-quality.yml` to build the VS Code bundle before
validation
- runs the new VS Code output validator as part of the main quality gate
- adds Cursor hook schema validation alongside the existing standalone
and smoke checks

- **Installer completeness**
- updates the VS Code install scripts to copy `vscode/mcp.json` into the
target repo’s `.vscode/mcp.json`
- closes the gap between documented install output and actual installed
files

- **Validator compatibility fixes**
- updates Cursor hook validation to allow environment-variable-based
command targets used by installed hooks
- keeps standalone audit coverage while avoiding false positives on the
new validation script

Example of the new validation entrypoint:

```bash
node ./scripts/validate-vscode-output.js
node ./scripts/validate-vscode-output.js --project-root "/path/to/copied-repo"
```

commit.message:
Remove IHostApplicationBuilder inheritance from IClientBuilder (#57432)

* Remove IHostApplicationBuilder inheritance from IClientBuilder

IClientBuilder now uses composition instead of inheritance for
IHostApplicationBuilder. The internal ClientBuilder still wraps
an IHostApplicationBuilder via its _host field.

This prevents chaining multiple AddClient calls
(builder.AddClient<A>().AddClient<B>()) but preserves the
builder.AddClient<A>().WithAzureCredential() pattern since
AddClient, PostConfigure, and WithAzureCredential all return
IClientBuilder.

AddAzureClient, AddKeyedAzureClient, and WithAzureCredential
return types changed from IHostApplicationBuilder to IClientBuilder.

fix: route non-success streaming responses through buffered error path (#1348)

## Summary

When a streaming request (`stream: true` in OpenAI Chat Completions, or
`stream: true` in Anthropic Messages) receives a non-2xx error response
from the backend, the error response body is silently dropped. The
client receives the correct status code and headers (including
`Content-Length`) but zero body bytes, causing `unexpected EOF` /
`connection closed before message completed`.

## Root Cause

`process_response` dispatches to `process_streaming` based solely on
`req.streaming`, without checking the response status:

```rust
if req.streaming {
    return self.process_streaming(req, ..., resp).await;
}
```

Backend error responses (4xx, 5xx) are plain JSON (`application/json`),
not event-stream data. The streaming parsers — specifically the AWS
EventStream decoder for Bedrock — interpret the JSON bytes as binary
frame headers. For example, `{"me` (the start of `{"message":"..."}`')
is read as a big-endian u32 frame length of ~2 GB. The decoder returns
`Incomplete` forever, producing zero output bytes.

Meanwhile, the original `Content-Length` header from the backend is
preserved through `resp.map(|b| ...)`. The client sees `Content-Length:
116` but receives 0 bytes → `unexpected EOF`.

The non-streaming path correctly handles errors via `process_error` →
`translate_error`, but is unreachable when `req.streaming == true`.

## Fix

Add a response status check before entering the streaming path:

```rust
if req.streaming && resp.status().is_success() {
    return self.process_streaming(req, ..., resp).await;
}
```

Non-success responses now fall through to the buffered path where
`process_error` translates the backend error format to the frontend
format and the body is forwarded intact.

## Impact

This affects **every** streaming request to any backend that returns
plain JSON error responses — which is standard behavior for AWS Bedrock,
and likely other providers. Without this fix, clients cannot read error
details from streaming requests, making debugging backend rejections
(e.g. `ValidationException`) impossible.

Fixes #1340

---------

commit.message:
refactor: remove asyncio.run from scheduler thread (#318)

* refactor: remove asyncio.run from scheduler thread

The scheduler thread had its own asyncio.run() loop, but the asyncio.Lock
in db/core.py is tied to the main event loop (created by uvicorn via
standalone/launchers.py). Using the lock from two different loops causes
runtime errors.

Fix: capture the main event loop in start_scheduler() via
asyncio.get_running_loop() (safe since it's called from the async lifespan
context), pass it to SchedulerThread, and replace all async/await in
_run() and _try_schedule() with synchronous calls via
asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(..., self._loop).result().

The scheduler thread now blocks on each async call, which is fine since
the thread has no other work to do while waiting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [backend/test] verify fable output

* allow scheduler in integration tests

* Add first_run_override to schedule create/update; add execution integration test

- ScheduleSpecification and ScheduleUpdate gain an optional
  first_run_override: datetime field.  When supplied and not older than
  max_acceptable_delay_hours relative to current_scheduling_time(), it is
  used directly as the initial scheduled_at value instead of the next cron
  tick computed by calculate_next_run().
- _resolve_next_run() helper encapsulates the fallback logic in the router.
- update_schedule now also triggers a next-run recalculation when only
  first_run_override is provided (no cron/enabled change needed).
- tests/integration/utils.py gains ensure_schedule_run_v2(), which polls
  GET /schedule/runs until a run appears (configurable sleep/attempts,
  matching the style of ensure_completed_v2).
- test_schedule_v2_execute creates a full fable with a sink, schedules it
  with max_acceptable_delay_hours=1 and first_run_override 5 minutes in
  the past, waits for the scheduler to submit the job, then waits for
  completion and asserts the output file contains '85'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reject first_run_override when older than max_acceptable_delay_hours

If first_run_override is provided but its age exceeds max_acceptable_delay_hours,
raise HTTP 400 instead of silently falling back to the next cron tick.
Updated docstrings on ScheduleSpecification and ScheduleUpdate to reflect
the new error behaviour.

Skip safe-flatten for discriminator base models in FlattenPropertyVisitor (#57460)

flattens it, making the base type internal and breaking the polymorphic
hierarchy.

Changes:
- Add guard to skip safe-flatten when inner model has a discriminator property
- Remove dead internalProperty.IsDiscriminator check (a model-typed property
  cannot itself be a discriminator)

commit.message:
Remove AKS pre-check section from deploy scripts

The AKS reachability check (cluster existence, stopped-cluster prompt,
DNS resolution verification) ran before terraform plan, adding behavior
that CI/CD pipelines don't have. Terraform's kubectl provider already
uses try() wrappers to handle missing or stopped AKS gracefully.

Removed from both deploy.ps1 and deploy.sh:
- AKS cluster lookup via az aks show
- Stopped-cluster interactive prompt and az aks start
- DNS/FQDN resolution check
- AksClusterName / AKS_CLUSTER_NAME variables (only used by pre-check)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix daemon data sources: lazy handler lookup via runner reference

Data sources now hold a weak ref to DaemonAgentRunner and look up
githubToolsHandler/ragToolsHandler at fetch time, fixing the issue
where handlers were nil when captured at init (before MCPServerService
wired them).

Also:
- forceEvaluate bypasses empty events guard (runs even with no pending events)
- forceEvaluate bypasses 24h release notes cooldown in situation report
- Add data source diagnostics to daemon.status response
- Increase status report truncation from 2000 to 6000 chars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: allow deploy scripts to continue when AKS cluster doesn't exist yet

On first-time deploys, the AKS cluster won't exist. The pre-check was
incorrectly treating this as a fatal error. Since the kubectl provider
in providers.tf uses try() wrappers, Terraform can plan/apply without
AKS — kubectl resources simply won't apply until the cluster exists.

Changed both deploy.ps1 and deploy.sh to show an informational message
and continue instead of exiting with an error when AKS is not found.
Existing behavior for stopped clusters and DNS checks is unchanged.

fix: rename `currentRole` to `current-role` in health check response (#3291)

## Why make this change?

The `/health` endpoint response used `currentRole` (camelCase) while
every other multi-word key in the response uses kebab-case (`app-name`,
`cache-ttl-seconds`, `response-ms`, etc.), creating an inconsistent API
surface.

## What is this change?

- Updated `[JsonPropertyName("currentRole")]` →
`[JsonPropertyName("current-role")]` in
`ComprehensiveHealthCheckReport.cs`.

**Before:**
```json
{
  "status": "Healthy",
  "app-name": "dab_oss_2.0.0",
  "currentRole": "anonymous"
}
```

**After:**
```json
{
  "status": "Healthy",
  "app-name": "dab_oss_2.0.0",
  "current-role": "anonymous"
}
```

## How was this tested?

- [ ] Integration Tests
- [ ] Unit Tests

## Sample Request(s)

```http
GET /health
```
Response now contains `current-role` instead of `currentRole`.

fix: correct time format strings for Go's native time representation

The SQLiteTime scanner had broken format strings that used literal
+0000 and GMT instead of Go's format directives -0700 and MST.
This meant time.Now() values stored by modernc.org/sqlite could
not be parsed back, causing ValidateSession to silently fail and
return 401 on all authenticated requests.

Also fixed CSRF middleware path check (/auth/login -> /api/auth/login)
and auth middleware must-change-password allowlist paths.

commit.message:
fix: correct storage account firewall ordering in init scripts

Remove --default-action Deny from storage account creation and the
already-exists update path. Instead, detect the deployer IP, add it to
the storage firewall allow-list, create the tfstate container (with
error handling that exits on failure), and only then lock down with
--default-action Deny. Applied to both init.ps1 and init.sh for parity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: update CSRF and auth middleware paths to /api prefix

The CSRF middleware exempted /auth/login and /auth/logout from
CSRF checks, but routes now live under /api/. Login POST was
rejected with 403 because the path didn't match the exemption.

Same issue in auth middleware's must-change-password allowlist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Unix Launch Fixes (#4304)

* Gitignore for unix objects and bins

* libmpg123 mac

* try with kosmiskrisp instead of molten xx, much less problematic?

* Explicit cast for box3d and include for utlblockmemory to make clang happ

* Simple DLLImportResolver for the 3 platforms, on linux/mac it tries to load DLLs from managed/ directory even with PATH being set, and backport the steamworks behaviour from Facepunch.Steamwokrs

* Dont ShaderCompile outside of tools, resourcecompiler is not even enabled there

* Only enable steam audio binaural effect on windows, we self compile it with that feature on there, dont bother yet for linux/mac until we have a better way to do dependency track

* Fix DLLImportResolver, also let's try to keep just a single set of binaries for managed without platform variants

* Fix rect_t interop on ARM64

On ARM64, NativeRect (4 floats = HFA) gets passed in float registers (v0-v3), but the C++ function reads from x1 expecting a pointer → null crash.
On x64 Windows, passing a 16-byte struct "by value" through an unmanaged function pointer accidentally works because the x64 ABI passes it by hidden pointer anyway. On ARM64, NativeRect (4 floats = HFA) goes into float registers v0-v3, leaving x1=0x0 for the C++ Rect_t* parameter.

* Building the load path via string interpolation hardcodes "/" and can produce double-separators because NetCore.NativeDllPath already includes a trailing slash. For consistency with the rest of the codebase (e.g., InteropGen uses Path.Combine with NativeDllPath) and to avoid path quirks across platforms, use Path.Combine(NetCore.NativeDllPath, nativeName).

commit.message:
fix: avoid deadlock in ListUsers with single SQLite connection

ListUsers called GetRolesForUser inside the rows.Next() loop,
which tried to acquire a second database connection. With
SetMaxOpenConns(1), this caused a deadlock.

Fix: collect all users first, close the rows cursor, then
fetch roles in a separate loop.
